channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Roki Sasaki pulled from Dodger Stadium debut in 2nd inning after more struggles with his control
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Roki Sasaki's career with the Los Angeles Dodgers is not off to a sensational start. For a young pitcher who's been a dominant force at every other step in his meteoric baseball career, that's both a surprise and a challenge.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender people are about 1% of the US population. Yet they're a political lightning rod
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their fights often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: American woman held in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American woman detained for weeks in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released from custody, according to a person familiar with the matter and a social media post Saturday from a longtime U.S. diplomat.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Comedian Paul Rodriguez arrested on drug charge in California, police say
item.description: Rodriguez, 70, was booked at the Burbank Police Jail for narcotics possession and later released with a citation to appear in court on April 25, Sgt. Turner elaborated.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Plane crash near Minneapolis sends home up in flames with no survivors on board
item.description: Dramatic video captured the aftermath of a home being engulfed in flames near Minneapolis after a small plane crashed into the home on Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death of 90s sitcom actress being investigated by homicide detectives: report
item.description: Per TMZ, the woman has been identified as 58-year-old actress Cindyana Santangelo, best known for a role in "Married With Children.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan grandparents imprisoned in Mexico timeshare dispute as daughter begs for their release
item.description: Michigan couple Christy and Paul Akeo have been detained in a Cancun, Mexico, prison for weeks over a timeshare dispute. Their daughter is begging for their release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Feds on alert for Tesla 'Global Day of Action' after nationwide violence leads to arrests
item.description: A law enforcement expert shared how American residents can assist police in alerting and preventing violence during the Tesla Takedown Global Day of Action this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amanda Knox's advice for American linked to Punta Cana missing persons case
item.description: Amanda Knox was a student studying abroad in Italy when she was accused of murdering friend Meredith Kercher. She spent nearly four years in an Italian prison before she was exonerated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University's interim president steps down and returns to former post
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University’s interim president Katrina Armstrong has resigned, returning to her post running the New York school’s medical center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, pushing past opposition from dentists and national health organizations who warn the move will lead to medical problems that disproportionately affect low-income communities. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stryker armored vehicles deploy to US-Mexico border under Trump administration
item.description: The Pentagon is supporting U.S. Customs and Border Protection by deploying eight Stryker vehicles to deter and detect migrants at the U.S.-Mexico border near El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: David Childs, architect of One World Trade Center that rose on Twin Towers site after 9/11, dies
item.description: David Childs, the lead architect of the One World Trade Center skyscraper that rose from the site where the Twin Towers collapsed in New York City during the 9/11 attacks, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk to hand out $1M checks ahead of judicial race in Wisconsin
item.description: Elon Musk said he would be traveling to Wisconsin before the Supreme Court race to speak with voters and give out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell warned Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Waterbury woman pleads not guilty to holding stepson captive for decades
item.description: WATERBURY, Conn. (WTNH) — The Waterbury woman accused of holding her stepson captive for several decades pleaded not guilty in Waterbury Superior Court on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar, killer of Selena, denied parole
item.description: The woman convicted of killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Perez has been denied parole after spending decades behind bars for fatally shooting the young singer at a Texas motel in 1995, the state’s parole board announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5
